protocol.test_policy: test cubes evaluated only after internal selection and calibration; never used for tuning
protocol.internal_validation_limitation: seed-level validation within each development cube because only one cube per class is available
protocol.epoch_selection_rule: maximum development-validation macro-F1; ties use minimum NLL; exact ties retain the earliest epoch
protocol.shared_pairing: spectral_only and fusion_net use identical splits, seeds, spectral branch, classifier architecture, and initial spectral/classifier parameters

mat_csv_mean_consistency.purpose: representation integrity only; not model selection or tuning

primary_estimands.weighting: 8 class/source-cubes equal within each direction; two directions equal
8 class/source-cubes equal within each direction; two directions equal
8 class/source-cubes equal within each direction; two directions equal
8 class/source-cubes equal within each direction; two directions equal
8 class/source-cubes equal within each direction; two directions equal
8 class/source-cubes equal within each direction; two directions equal

spatial_mechanism_decision.decision_rule: both ensemble directional deltas positive; equal-weight ensemble mean delta >=0.02; and at least 5/6 direction-by-seed deltas positive

limitations: Each test class contains only one acquisition cube; seed predictions are clustered technical subsamples.
The reciprocal suffix test is not external farm, lot, harvest-year, supplier, or instrument validation.
Internal validation is seed-level within a cube and is used only for training control, not as generalization evidence.
Temperature calibration is learned on the internal validation seeds and may not transfer under cube shift.
Counterfactual effects diagnose model dependence but do not establish chemical causality.
